task_completion.description: Import the existing Gradle project into Eclipse
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Eclipse Project Explorer or Package Explorer shows the 'datautils' project, ideally with a Gradle icon 'G' indicating it was imported as a Gradle project.

task_completion.description: Add the Google Guava dependency to the build configuration
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'build.gradle' file is open in the editor showing 'implementation "com.google.guava:guava:33.0.0-jre"' (or similar configuration), or the Guava jar is visible under 'Project and External Dependencies' in the Project Explorer.

task_completion.description: Create the CollectionHelper class in the com.datautils.util package
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Project Explorer shows 'CollectionHelper.java' under 'src/main/java' -> 'com.datautils.util', or the file is open in the editor with the correct package declaration.
The editor shows a static method named 'immutableListOf' that takes a varargs parameter (T... elements) and returns an ImmutableList.
The editor shows a static method named 'immutableMapOf' that takes four parameters (K k1, V v1, K k2, V v2) and returns an ImmutableMap.

task_completion.description: Implement the concat method using an ImmutableList builder
task_completion.visual_evidence: The editor shows a static method named 'concat' that takes two List parameters and uses 'ImmutableList.builder()' to combine them and return an ImmutableList.

task_completion.description: Verify that the project builds successfully
task_completion.visual_evidence: There are no red error markers (x icons) on the 'datautils' project or its files in the Project Explorer, and the Problems view does not show any Java compilation errors.

integrity.description: The agent used the Eclipse IDE to perform the tasks
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Eclipse IDE interface being used for project management and code editing, rather than terminal-based text editors like nano or vim.

integrity.description: The agent manually entered or edited the code in the IDE
integrity.visual_evidence: The code editor in Eclipse shows the actual implementation of the CollectionHelper class, rather than just running a pre-written script in the terminal to generate the files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The project is located at /home/ga/projects/datautils. The expected dependency is exactly 'com.google.guava:guava:33.0.0-jre'. The class should be created at src/main/java/com/datautils/util/CollectionHelper.java. The expected methods must use Guava's com.google.common.collect.ImmutableList and ImmutableMap. The concat method must specifically use ImmutableList.builder().
